templates.name: GitHub repository by HTTP
templates.description:
This template is designed for the effortless deployment of GitHub repository monitoring by Zabbix via GitHub REST API and doesn't require any external scripts.

For more details about GitHub REST API, refer to the official documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest?apiVersion=2022-11-28

Setup:

1. Create an access token for monitoring

One of the simplest ways to send authenticated requests is to use a personal access token - either a classic or a fine-grained one:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/authentication/authenticating-to-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token

Classic personal access token

You can create a new classic personal access token by following the instructions in the official documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens#creating-a-personal-access-token-classic

For public repositories, no additional permission scopes are required. For monitoring to work on private repositories, the "repo" scope must be set to have full control of private repositories.

Additional information about OAuth scopes is available in the official documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/scopes-for-oauth-apps#available-scopes

Note that authenticated users must have admin access to the repository and the "repo" scope must be set to get information about self-hosted runners:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/self-hosted-runners?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-self-hosted-runners-for-a-repository

Fine-grained personal access token

Alternatively, you can use a fine-grained personal access token:
https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens#creating-a-fine-grained-personal-access-token

In order to use fine-grained tokens to monitor organization-owned repositories, organizations must opt in to fine-grained personal access tokens and set up a personal access token policy:
https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-programmatic-access-to-your-organization/setting-a-personal-access-token-policy-for-your-organization

The fine-grained token needs to have the following permissions set to provide access to the repository resources:
- "Actions" repository permissions (read);
- "Administration" repository permissions (read);
- "Contents" repository permissions (read);
- "Issues" repository permissions (read);
- "Metadata" repository permissions (read);
- "Pull requests" repository permissions (read).

2. Set the access token that you've created in step 1 in the "{$GITHUB.API.TOKEN}" macro
3. Change the API URL in the "{$GITHUB.API.URL}" macro if needed (for self-hosted installations)
4. Set the repository owner name in the "{$GITHUB.REPO.OWNER}" macro
5. Set the repository name in the "{$GITHUB.REPO.NAME}" macro
6. Set the LLD rule filters if needed (you may want to use it to also stay within rate limits as on large repositories, LLD rules may generate a lot of script items)

Note: Update intervals and timeouts for script items can be changed individually via "{$GITHUB.INTERVAL}" and "{$GITHUB.TIMEOUT}" macros with context. Depending on the repository being monitored, it can be adjusted if needed (if you are exceeding rate limits, you can increase update intervals for some script items to stay within per hour request limits). But be aware that it may also affect the triggers (check whether the item is used in triggers and adjust thresholds and/or evaluation periods if needed).

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"
templates.readme:
## Overview

This template is designed for the effortless deployment of GitHub repository monitoring by Zabbix via GitHub REST API and doesn't require any external scripts.

For more details about GitHub REST API, refer to the [official documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/rest?apiVersion=2022-11-28).

## Setup

GitHub limits the number of REST API requests that you can make within a specific amount of time, which also depends on whether you are authenticated or not, the plan, and the token type used. Many REST API endpoints require authentication or return additional information if you are authenticated. Additionally, you can make more requests per hour when you are authenticated.

Additional information is available in the official documentation:
- [Regarding authentication](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/authentication/authenticating-to-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28#about-authentication)
- [Rate limits for the REST API](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28)

1. **Create an access token for monitoring**

One of the simplest ways to send authenticated requests is to use a [personal access token](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/authentication/authenticating-to-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token) - either a classic or a fine-grained one.

**Classic personal access token**

You can create a new classic personal access token by following the [instructions in the official documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens#creating-a-personal-access-token-classic).

For public repositories, no additional permission scopes are required. For monitoring to work on private repositories, the `repo` scope must be set to have full control of private repositories.

Additional information about OAuth scopes is available in the [official documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/scopes-for-oauth-apps#available-scopes).

Note that authenticated users must have admin access to the repository and the `repo` scope must be set to get information about [self-hosted runners](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/self-hosted-runners?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-self-hosted-runners-for-a-repository).

**Fine-grained personal access token**

Alternatively, you can use a [fine-grained personal access token](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens#creating-a-fine-grained-personal-access-token).

In order to use fine-grained tokens to monitor organization-owned repositories, [organizations must opt in to fine-grained personal access tokens and set up a personal access token policy](https://docs.github.com/en/organizations/managing-programmatic-access-to-your-organization/setting-a-personal-access-token-policy-for-your-organization).

The fine-grained token needs to have the following permissions set to provide access to the repository resources:
- "Actions" repository permissions (read);
- "Administration" repository permissions (read);
- "Contents" repository permissions (read);
- "Issues" repository permissions (read);
- "Metadata" repository permissions (read);
- "Pull requests" repository permissions (read).

2. **Set the access token** that you've created in step 1 in the `Access token` host wizard configuration field
3. **Change the API URL** in the `API URL` host wizard configuration field if needed (for self-hosted installations)
4. **Set the repository owner name** in the `Repository owner` host wizard configuration field
5. **Set the repository name** in the `Repository name` host wizard configuration field
6. **Set the LLD rule filters** if needed (you may want to use them to stay within rate limits as on large repositories, LLD rules may generate a lot of script items):
- Filter repository branches by name: `Branch name filter (include)`, `Branch name filter (exclude)`;
- Filter repository workflows by name: `Workflow name filter (include)`, `Workflow name filter (exclude)`;
- Filter repository workflows by state: `Workflow state filter (include)`, `Workflow state filter (exclude)`;
- Filter self-hosted runners by name: `Self-hosted runner name filter (include)`, `Self-hosted runner name filter (exclude)`;
- Filter self-hosted runners by OS: `Self-hosted runner OS filter (include)`, `Self-hosted runner OS filter (exclude)`.

Note: Update intervals and timeouts for script items can be changed individually via `Data update interval` and `API timeout` host wizard configuration fields with context. Depending on the repository being monitored, it can be adjusted if needed (if you are exceeding rate limits, you can increase update intervals for some script items to stay within per hour request limits). But be aware that it may also affect the triggers (check whether the item is used in triggers and adjust thresholds and/or evaluation periods if needed).

items.name: Get branches
items.description:
Get the repository branches.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/branches/branches?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-branches

items.name: Number of closed issues
items.description: The number of closed issues in the repository.

items.name: Get issue count
items.description:
Get the count of issues and pull requests in the repository (total, open, closed).

Information about endpoint for issues:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/issues/issues?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-repository-issues

Information about endpoint for pull requests:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/pulls/pulls?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-pull-requests

items.name: Number of open issues
items.description: The number of open issues in the repository.

items.name: Number of issues
items.description: The total number of issues in the repository.

triggers.expression: last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.issues.total)>last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.issues.total,#2)
triggers.name: GitHub: The total number of issues has increased
triggers.description: The total number of issues has increased which means that either a new issue (or multiple) has been opened.

items.name: Number of closed PRs
items.description: The number of closed pull requests in the repository.

items.name: Number of open PRs
items.description: The number of open pull requests in the repository.

items.name: Number of PRs
items.description: The total number of pull requests in the repository.

triggers.expression: last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.pr.total)>last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.pr.total,#2)
triggers.name: GitHub: The total number of PRs has increased
triggers.description: The total number of pull requests has increased which means that either a new pull request (or multiple) has been opened.

items.name: Number of releases
items.description:
The number of releases in the repository. Note that this number also includes draft releases.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/releases?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-releases

items.name: Repository forks
items.description: The number of repository forks.

items.name: Get repository
items.description:
Get the general repository information. If the repository is not a fork, the community profile metrics are also retrieved.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/repos/repos?apiVersion=2022-11-28#get-a-repository

Information about community profile metrics:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/metrics/community?apiVersion=2022-11-28#get-community-profile-metrics

items.name: Get repository data check
items.description: Data collection check.

triggers.name: GitHub: There are errors in requests to API
triggers.description: Errors have been received in response to API requests. Check the latest values for details.

items.name: Repository is a fork
items.description: Indicates whether the repository is a fork.

items.name: Repository size
items.description: The size of the repository.

items.name: Repository stargazers
items.description: The number of GitHub users who have starred the repository.

items.name: Repository watchers
items.description: The number of GitHub users who are subscribed to the repository.

items.name: Request limit
items.description:
API request limit.

Information about request limits in GitHub REST API documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28

items.name: Requests used
items.description:
The number of used API requests.

Information about request limits in GitHub REST API documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28

items.name: Request limit utilization, in %
items.description:
The calculated utilization of the API request limit in %.

Information about request limits in GitHub REST API documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28

triggers.name: GitHub: API request limit utilization is high
triggers.event_name: GitHub: API request limit utilization is high (usage is > {$GITHUB.REQUESTS.UTIL.WARN})
triggers.description:
The API request limit utilization is high. It can be lowered by increasing the update intervals for script items (by setting up higher values in corresponding context macros).

The trigger will be resolved automatically if the limit usage drops 5% below the trigger threshold.

Information about request limits in GitHub REST API documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28

dependencies.name: GitHub: API request limit utilization is very high

triggers.name: GitHub: API request limit utilization is very high
triggers.event_name: GitHub: API request limit utilization is very high (usage is > {$GITHUB.REQUESTS.UTIL.HIGH})
triggers.description:
The API request limit utilization is very high. It can be lowered by increasing the update intervals for script items (by setting up higher values in corresponding context macros).

The trigger will be resolved automatically if the limit usage drops 5% below the trigger threshold.

Information about request limits in GitHub REST API documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28

items.name: Get self-hosted runners
items.description:
Get the self-hosted runners of the repository.

Note that admin access to the repository is required to use this endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/self-hosted-runners?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-self-hosted-runners-for-a-repository

items.name: Get self-hosted runner check
items.description: Carry out a self-hosted runners data collection check.

triggers.expression: find(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.runners.get.check,,"iregexp","Must have admin rights to Repository")=1
triggers.name: GitHub: No access to repository self-hosted runners
triggers.description:
Admin access to the repository is required to use this endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/self-hosted-runners?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-self-hosted-runners-for-a-repository

items.name: Number of tags
items.description:
The number of tags in the repository.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/repos/repos?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-repository-tags

items.name: Get workflows
items.description:
Get the repository workflows.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/workflows?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-repository-workflows

discovery_rules.name: Branch discovery
discovery_rules.description:
Discovers repository branches.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/branches/branches?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-branches

item_prototypes.name: Branch [{#BRANCH_NAME}]: Number of commits
item_prototypes.description:
Get the number of commits in the branch.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/commits/commits?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-commits

discovery_rules.name: Discovery of community profile metrics
discovery_rules.description:
Discovers community profile metrics (the repository must not be a fork).

Information about community profile metrics:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/metrics/community?apiVersion=2022-11-28#get-community-profile-metrics

item_prototypes.name: Health percentage score
item_prototypes.description:
The health percentage score is defined as a percentage of how many of the recommended community health files are present.

For more information, see the documentation:
https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions/about-community-profiles-for-public-repositories

discovery_rules.name: Self-hosted runner discovery
discovery_rules.description:
Discovers self-hosted runners of the repository.

Note that admin access to the repository is required to use this endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/self-hosted-runners?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-self-hosted-runners-for-a-repository

item_prototypes.name: Runner [{#RUNNER_NAME}]: Busy
item_prototypes.description: Indicates whether the runner is currently executing a job.

item_prototypes.name: Runner [{#RUNNER_NAME}]: Online
item_prototypes.description: Indicates whether the runner is connected to GitHub and is ready to execute jobs.

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.runners.online[{#RUNNER_NAME}],#2)=1 and last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.runners.online[{#RUNNER_NAME}])=0
trigger_prototypes.name: GitHub: Runner [{#RUNNER_NAME}]: The runner has become offline
trigger_prototypes.description: The runner was online previously, but is currently not connected to GitHub. This could be because the machine is offline, the self-hosted runner application is not running on the machine, or the self-hosted runner application cannot communicate with GitHub.

discovery_rules.name: Workflow discovery
discovery_rules.description:
Discovers repository workflows. By default, only the active workflows are discovered.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/workflows?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-repository-workflows

item_prototypes.name: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: Last run conclusion
item_prototypes.description:
The conclusion of the last workflow run. Possible values:

0 - success
1 - failure
2 - neutral
3 - cancelled
4 - skipped
5 - timed_out
6 - action_required
10 - unknown

trigger_prototypes.name: GitHub: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: The workflow has failed
trigger_prototypes.description: The last workflow run has returned a "failure" conclusion.

item_prototypes.name: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: Last run duration
item_prototypes.description: The duration of the last workflow run.

item_prototypes.name: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: Get last run
item_prototypes.description:
Get the data about the last workflow run.

Information about endpoint:
https://docs.github.com/en/rest/actions/workflow-runs?apiVersion=2022-11-28#list-workflow-runs-for-a-workflow

item_prototypes.name: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: Last run start date
item_prototypes.description: The date when the last workflow run was started.

item_prototypes.name: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: Last run status
item_prototypes.description:
The status of the last workflow run. Possible values:

0 - queued
1 - in_progress
2 - completed
10 - unknown

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.workflows.last_run.status[{#WORKFLOW_NAME}])=1 and changecount(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.workflows.last_run.status[{#WORKFLOW_NAME}],{$GITHUB.WORKFLOW.STATUS.IN_PROGRESS.THRESH:"workflow_in_progress:{#WORKFLOW_NAME}"})=0
trigger_prototypes.name: GitHub: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: The workflow has been in progress for too long
trigger_prototypes.event_name: GitHub: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: The workflow has been in progress for too long (over {$GITHUB.WORKFLOW.STATUS.IN_PROGRESS.THRESH:"workflow_in_progress:{#WORKFLOW_NAME}"})
trigger_prototypes.description: The last workflow run has been in the "in_progress" status for too long. The default threshold is provided as an example and can be adjusted for relevant workflows with context macros.

trigger_prototypes.expression: last(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.workflows.last_run.status[{#WORKFLOW_NAME}])=0 and changecount(/GitHub repository by HTTP/github.repo.workflows.last_run.status[{#WORKFLOW_NAME}],{$GITHUB.WORKFLOW.STATUS.QUEUED.THRESH:"workflow_queued:{#WORKFLOW_NAME}"})=0
trigger_prototypes.name: GitHub: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: The workflow has been in the queue for too long
trigger_prototypes.event_name: GitHub: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: The workflow has been in the queue for too long (over {$GITHUB.WORKFLOW.STATUS.QUEUED.THRESH:"workflow_queued:{#WORKFLOW_NAME}"})
trigger_prototypes.description: The last workflow run has been in the "queued" status for too long. This may mean that it has failed to be assigned to a runner. The default threshold is provided as an example and can be adjusted for relevant workflows with context macros.

item_prototypes.name: Workflow [{#WORKFLOW_NAME}]: Last run update date
item_prototypes.description: The date when the last workflow run was updated.

macros.description: Set the access token here.

config.label: Access token
config.description: Set the access token here.

macros.description: Set the API URL here.

config.label: API URL
config.description: Set the API URL here.

macros.description: The API version that is used in headers for HTTP requests.

config.label: API version
config.description: The API version that is used in headers for HTTP requests.

macros.description: The repository branch name regex filter to use in branch-related metric discovery - for including.

config.label: Branch name filter (include)
config.description: The repository branch name regex filter to use in branch-related metric discovery - for including.

macros.description: The repository branch name regex filter to use in branch-related metric discovery - for excluding.

config.label: Branch name filter (exclude)
config.description: The repository branch name regex filter to use in branch-related metric discovery - for excluding.

macros.description: The HTTP proxy for script items (set if needed). If the macro is empty, then no proxy is used.

config.label: HTTP proxy
config.description: The HTTP proxy for script items (set if needed). If the macro is empty, then no proxy is used.

macros.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve data from the API. Can be used with context if needed (check the context values in relevant items).

config.label: Data update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve data from the API. Can be used with context if needed (check the context values in relevant items).

macros.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the branches and workflows. Used only for related metric discovery.

config.label: Branches/workflows update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the branches and workflows. Used only for related metric discovery.

macros.description: The update interval for the script item that retrieves the repository information.

config.label: Repository info update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script item that retrieves the repository information.

macros.description: The update interval for the script item that retrieves the information about self-hosted runners.

config.label: Self-hosted runners info update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script item that retrieves the information about self-hosted runners.

macros.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the number of tags, releases, issues, and pull requests (total, open, closed).

config.label: Tags/releases/issues update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the number of tags, releases, issues, and pull requests (total, open, closed).

macros.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the commits count in discovered branches.

config.label: Commits count update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the commits count in discovered branches.

macros.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the information about the last workflow run results.

config.label: Workflow run results update interval
config.description: The update interval for the script items that retrieve the information about the last workflow run results.

macros.description: Set the repository name here.

config.label: Repository name
config.description: Set the repository name here.

macros.description: Set the repository owner here.

config.label: Repository owner
config.description: Set the repository owner here.

macros.description: The threshold percentage of utilized API requests in a High trigger expression.

config.label: API usage high threshold (%)
config.description: The threshold percentage of utilized API requests in a High trigger expression. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: The threshold percentage of utilized API requests in a Warning trigger expression.

config.label: API usage warning threshold (%)
config.description: The threshold percentage of utilized API requests in a Warning trigger expression. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: The number of results to fetch per page. Can be used with context and adjusted if needed (check the context values in script parameters of relevant items).

config.label: Results per page
config.description: The number of results to fetch per page. Can be used with context and adjusted if needed (check the context values in script parameters of relevant items).

macros.description: The repository self-hosted runner name regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for including.

config.label: Self-hosted runner name filter (include)
config.description: The repository self-hosted runner name regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for including.

macros.description: The repository self-hosted runner name regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for excluding.

config.label: Self-hosted runner name filter (exclude)
config.description: The repository self-hosted runner name regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for excluding.

macros.description: The repository self-hosted runner OS regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for including.

config.label: Self-hosted runner OS filter (include)
config.description: The repository self-hosted runner OS regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for including.

macros.description: The repository self-hosted runner OS regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for excluding.

config.label: Self-hosted runner OS filter (exclude)
config.description: The repository self-hosted runner OS regex filter to use in discovering metrics related to the self-hosted runner - for excluding.

macros.description: The timeout threshold for the script items that retrieve data from the API. Can be used with context if needed (check the context values in relevant items).

config.label: API timeout
config.description: The timeout threshold for the script items that retrieve data from the API. Can be used with context if needed (check the context values in relevant items).

macros.description: The user agent that is used in headers for HTTP requests.

config.label: HTTP user agent
config.description: The user agent that is used in headers for HTTP requests.

macros.description: The repository workflow name regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for including.

config.label: Workflow name filter (include)
config.description: The repository workflow name regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for including.

macros.description: The repository workflow name regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for excluding.

config.label: Workflow name filter (exclude)
config.description: The repository workflow name regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for excluding.

macros.description: The repository workflow state regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for including.

config.label: Workflow state filter (include)
config.description: The repository workflow state regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for including.

macros.description: The repository workflow state regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for excluding.

config.label: Workflow state filter (exclude)
config.description: The repository workflow state regex filter to use in workflow-related metric discovery - for excluding.

macros.description: The time threshold used in the trigger of a workflow run that has been in the queue for too long. Can be used with context if needed.

config.label: Workflow queue time high threshold
config.description: The time threshold used in the trigger of a workflow run that has been in the queue for too long. Can be used with context if needed.

macros.description: The time threshold used in the trigger of a workflow run that has been in the queue for too long. Can be used with context if needed.

config.label: Workflow queue time warning threshold
config.description: The time threshold used in the trigger of a workflow run that has been in the queue for too long. Can be used with context if needed.
